title: Best Real Estate Podcasts and YouTube Channels for Beginners (2026 Edition)
excerpt: Discover the top real estate podcasts and YouTube channels that help beginners learn wholesaling, flipping, and investing faster. We break down exactly what each show covers so you can start learning today.
author: AIWholesail Team
tags: best real estate podcasts for beginners
metaDescription: Find the best real estate podcasts and YouTube channels for beginners in 2026. Learn wholesaling, flipping, and investing from the top creators.
metaKeywords: best real estate podcasts for beginners, real estate YouTube channels, wholesale real estate podcast, real estate investing podcast, beginner real estate education, BiggerPockets podcast, real estate YouTube 2026

sections.content: Getting started in real estate can feel overwhelming. There are contracts to understand, markets to analyze, and strategies to learn. The good news? Some of the smartest investors in the country are sharing everything they know for free through podcasts and YouTube. Whether you are commuting to work, walking the dog, or sitting at your desk, you can be absorbing real-world education that shortens your learning curve by years. If you are brand new, start with our [Wholesale Real Estate for Beginners: Complete Guide](aiwholesail.com/guides/wholesale-real-estate-beginners-guide) to build your foundation, then use this list to go deeper on the topics that matter most to you.
Why Free Content Is a Legitimate Real Estate Education

Many successful wholesalers and investors never paid for a $5,000 guru course. They listened to hundreds of podcast episodes, watched YouTube walkthroughs, and then took action. Free content has exploded in quality over the last five years. Today you can find detailed breakdowns of actual deals, interviews with investors doing 50 or 100 deals a year, and step-by-step tutorials on everything from writing an offer to running comps. The key is knowing which shows to trust and what each one covers best.
Top Real Estate Podcasts for Beginners
1. BiggerPockets Real Estate Podcast

BiggerPockets is the gold standard for beginner real estate education. The show has over 900 episodes covering rental properties, flipping, wholesaling, and creative financing. Hosts Dave Meyer and Rob Abasolo interview investors at every level, from first-timers to people with hundreds of units. Episodes are conversational and easy to follow. Start with the 'Rookie' episodes if you have never bought a deal before. The Rookie Podcast is actually a separate feed from BiggerPockets focused entirely on people doing their first one to ten deals.
2. Wholesaling Inc. Podcast

If your focus is wholesaling specifically, this is your show. Brent Daniels and the Wholesaling Inc. team publish multiple episodes per week featuring real investors closing real deals. You will hear stories of people finding their first deal with zero money down — something we dig into in our guide on [Wholesale Real Estate With No Money: Is It Possible?](aiwholesail.com/blog/wholesale-real-estate-no-money). The podcast is practical and tactical. Episodes often break down specific marketing strategies like cold calling, driving for dollars, and direct mail.
3. Real Estate Rookie Podcast

This BiggerPockets spin-off is hosted by Ashley Kehr and Tony Robinson and focuses entirely on investors who are just starting out. The hosts share their own mistakes openly, which makes the advice feel real and relatable. Episodes run about 45 to 60 minutes and are organized around specific beginner questions like how much earnest money to put up — a topic you can read more about in our [Wholesale Real Estate Earnest Money: How Much and When](aiwholesail.com/blog/wholesale-earnest-money-guide) guide.
4. The Real Estate Guys Radio Show

This long-running show has been on the air since 1997 and covers both domestic and international investing. It is especially strong on market cycles, economic trends, and passive investing. If you want the bigger picture on where markets like [Atlanta](aiwholesail.com/markets/atlanta-ga), [Dallas](aiwholesail.com/markets/dallas-tx), and [Phoenix](aiwholesail.com/markets/phoenix-az) are headed, the Real Estate Guys provide context that helps you make smarter buying decisions.
5. On The Market by BiggerPockets

This show focuses on data, market trends, and economic analysis. Dave Meyer and a rotating panel of experts break down interest rate shifts, housing inventory numbers, and where the best opportunities are emerging. It is a great complement to the more tactical shows because it helps you understand the why behind market movements.

Pro Tip: Do not try to listen to all five podcasts at once. Pick one that matches your current strategy, listen to 10 to 15 episodes, then take one action based on what you learned before adding more content to your rotation.
Best Real Estate YouTube Channels for Beginners
1. BiggerPockets (YouTube)

The BiggerPockets YouTube channel has over 1.4 million subscribers and uploads multiple videos each week. The content ranges from five-minute explainers on cap rates to two-hour deep dives into specific investing strategies. One of the most valuable playlist types they offer is the 'deal breakdown' format where an investor walks through an actual property, explains the numbers, and shows what they would do with it.
2. Wholesale Hotline by Brent Daniels

Brent Daniels runs a high-energy YouTube channel built around his TTP (Talk To People) cold calling method. He frequently posts live cold calls, which is something most educators never show. Watching someone actually talk to a motivated seller in real time is more educational than most written guides. If you are nervous about talking to sellers, this channel will help you build confidence fast.
3. Max Maxwell

Max Maxwell is a North Carolina-based wholesaler who built his business from scratch and now documents deals, marketing strategies, and the business side of wholesaling in detail. His channel is particularly useful for understanding how to structure your business, manage a small team, and scale beyond your first few deals.
4. Flipping Mastery TV by Jerry Norton

Jerry Norton's channel is excellent for flippers and those who want to understand renovation budgets, the ARV (after repair value) process, and how to estimate rehab costs. He uploads consistently and often shares full deal walkthroughs in specific markets. If you are trying to understand how to run numbers on a property, pair this channel with AIWholesail's free [Deal Calculator](aiwholesail.com/tools/wholesale-deal-calculator) to practice on real numbers.
5. Sean Pan Real Estate

Sean Pan covers rental property investing with a focus on cash flow and using loans creatively. His content is beginner-friendly and he explains financial concepts clearly without assuming prior knowledge. If passive income through buy-and-hold investing interests you, his channel is a strong starting point.
How to Use These Resources Without Getting Stuck in Learning Mode

The biggest trap in real estate education is endless consumption with zero action. It is sometimes called 'analysis paralysis.' You can listen to 500 podcast episodes and still feel like you are not ready. Here is a structured approach to avoid that trap and actually move forward.

items: Week 1-2: Pick one strategy. Read our [Real Estate Investing for Beginners](aiwholesail.com/guides/real-estate-investing-for-beginners) guide and choose between wholesaling, flipping, or buy-and-hold rentals.
Week 3-4: Subscribe to one podcast and one YouTube channel focused on that strategy. Listen to or watch 10 episodes.
Week 5-6: Take a specific action. Drive for dollars, pull a list, or call three local investors to introduce yourself.
Ongoing: Use what you are learning in real situations. Every week you should be doing something, not just consuming content.
When legal questions come up: Review our state-by-state breakdown on [Is Wholesale Real Estate Legal?](aiwholesail.com/blog/is-wholesale-real-estate-legal-state-guide) before signing any contracts.
When running numbers: Use the [Wholesale Deal Calculator](aiwholesail.com/tools/wholesale-deal-calculator) on every property you evaluate to build the habit of analyzing before you offer.

sections.content: 'The best investment you can make is in your own education — but only if it leads to action. One deal closed is worth a thousand hours of podcasts listened.' — A mindset shared consistently across the best real estate educators in the space.
What to Listen For as a Beginner

When you are new, every episode feels like you are drinking from a fire hose. Instead of trying to absorb everything, listen for three specific things: how investors find deals, how they analyze whether a deal is good, and how they fund the transaction. Those three pillars cover most of what you need to do your first deal. Everything else — scaling, systems, teams — comes later. If you want a deeper structure for how all of this fits together, our [Wholesaling Real Estate for Beginners](aiwholesail.com/guides/wholesaling-real-estate-for-beginners) guide walks through each step in order.

Pro Tip: Keep a simple notes document on your phone. Every time a podcast guest mentions a specific tactic, number, or resource, write it down. Review your notes once a week and pick one thing to research or try.

Ready to put your education into practice? AIWholesail helps you find motivated seller leads and analyze deals in real time — so you can move from learning to closing faster.
